27 His cloak is the only covering he has for his body. What else will he sleep in? If he cries out to me, I will hear him. I am full of mercy.
28 You must not speak evil of God. You must not curse the leader of your people.
29 You must not hold back offerings from your storehouses or wine jars. You are to give me the firstborn of your sons.
30 Do the same with your cattle and your sheep. Let them stay with their mothers for seven days. On the eighth day, you must give them to me.
31 You are to be my holy people. You must not eat the meat of an animal killed by wild animals in the field. Throw it to the dogs."

title: Exodus 23
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 "Do not spread a false report. Do not help the wicked by being a lying witness.
2 Do not follow the crowd in doing wrong. In court, do not twist what is right by siding with the crowd.
3 And do not take sides with a poor man in his case.
4 If you find your enemy's ox or donkey that has wandered off, take it back to him.
5 If you see the donkey of someone who hates you fallen under its load, do not leave it. You must help him with it.
6 Do not refuse justice to the poor in their cases.
7 Stay far away from a false charge. Do not kill the innocent or the just. I will not let the guilty go free.
8 Do not accept a bribe. A bribe blinds those who see and twists the words of the righteous.
9 Do not oppress a foreigner. You know how it feels to be foreigners. You were foreigners in the land of Egypt.
10 For six years, plant your land and gather its crops.
11 But in the seventh year, let the land rest with no planting. Then the poor among your people may eat from it. The wild animals may eat what is left. Do the same with your vineyard and olive grove.
12 For six days, do your work. But on the seventh day, you must stop. Then your ox and your donkey can rest. Your servant's son and the foreigner can be refreshed too.
13 Pay close attention to everything I have said to you. Do not call on the names of other gods. Do not let their names be heard on your lips.
14 Three times a year, you are to hold a feast to Me.

15 Keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread, as I commanded you. At the set time in the month of Abib, eat unleavened bread for seven days. That was the month you came out of Egypt. No one may come before Me with empty hands.

16 Also keep the Feast of Harvest, with the firstfruits of the crops you plant in the field. And keep the Feast of Ingathering at the end of the year. That is when you gather your crops from the field.
17 Three times a year, all your men are to appear before the Lord GOD.
18 Do not offer the blood of My sacrifices with anything that has yeast. Do not let the fat of My feast remain until morning.
19 Bring the best of the firstfruits of your soil to the house of the LORD your God. Do not cook a young goat in its mother's milk.
20 "Look, I am sending an angel before you. He will guard you along the way. He will bring you to the place I have prepared.
21 Pay attention to him. Listen to what he says. Do not rebel against him. He will not forgive your rebellion, since my Name is in him.
22 But if you listen carefully to his voice and do everything I say, I will be an enemy to your enemies. I will fight against those who fight you.
23 My angel will go before you. He will bring you into the land of the Amorites, Hittites, Perizzites, Canaanites, Hivites, and Jebusites. And I will wipe them out.
24 Do not bow down to their gods or serve them. Do not follow their ways. Instead, tear down their altars and break their sacred stones.
25 So you shall serve the LORD your God. He will bless your bread and your water. I will take away sickness from among you.
26 No woman in your land will lose a baby or be unable to have children. I will give you a full number of days.
27 I will send My fear ahead of you. I will throw every nation you meet into confusion. I will make all your enemies turn and run.
28 I will send the hornet ahead of you. It will drive the Hivites, Canaanites, and Hittites out of your way.
29 I will not drive them out in a single year. If I did, the land would become empty. The wild animals would grow too many for you.
30 Little by little I will drive them out before you, until you have increased enough to take possession of the land.

31 I will set your borders from the Red Sea to the Mediterranean Sea. They will run from the desert to the Euphrates River. I will hand the people of the land over to you. You will drive them out before you.
32 Do not make any covenant with them or with their gods.
33 They must not stay in your land. If they do, they will make you sin against Me. If you serve their gods, it will surely be a trap for you."

title: Exodus 24
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses, "Come up to the LORD. Bring Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and seventy of Israel's elders. You must worship from far away.
2 Only Moses may come near the LORD. The others must not come close. And the people must not go up with him."
3 Moses came and told the people all the LORD's words and rules. They all answered with one voice, "We will do everything the LORD has said."
4 Moses wrote down all the words of the LORD. Early the next morning, he got up. He built an altar at the base of the mountain. He also set up twelve stone pillars for the twelve tribes of Israel.
5 Then he sent some young men of Israel. They offered burnt offerings to the LORD. They also sacrificed young bulls as fellowship offerings.
6 Moses took half of the blood and put it in bowls. He sprinkled the other half on the altar.
7 Then he took the Book of the Covenant and read it to the people. They replied, "We will do everything the LORD has said; we will obey."
8 So Moses took the blood and sprinkled it on the people. He said, "This is the blood of the covenant that the LORD has made with you, based on all these words."
9 Then Moses went up with Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel.
10 And they saw the God of Israel. Under his feet was something like a pavement made of lapis lazuli, as bright as the sky.
11 But God did not raise his hand against the leaders of Israel. They saw him, and they ate and drank.
12 Then the LORD said to Moses, "Come up to me on the mountain and stay here. I will give you the tablets of stone. They have the law and commandments I have written to teach the people."
13 So Moses set out with Joshua his helper. They went up on the mountain of God.
14 Moses said to the elders, "Wait here for us until we come back to you. Aaron and Hur are here with you. Anyone who has a problem to settle can go to them."
15 When Moses went up on the mountain, the cloud covered it.
16 The glory of the LORD rested on Mount Sinai. For six days the cloud covered the mountain. On the seventh day, the LORD called to Moses from inside the cloud.
17 To the Israelites, the appearance of the glory of the LORD was like a consuming fire on the mountaintop.
18 Moses entered the cloud as he went up on the mountain. He stayed on the mountain forty days and forty nights.

title: Exodus 25
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Tell the Israelites to bring me an offering. Receive my offering from every person whose heart moves him to give.
3 This is the offering you are to take from them: gold, silver, and bronze;
4 blue, purple, and scarlet yarn; fine linen and goat hair;
5 ram skins dyed red and fine leather; acacia wood;
6 olive oil for the light; spices for the anointing oil and for the sweet incense;
7 and onyx stones and other gemstones to set in the ephod and breastpiece.
8 Have them make a sanctuary for me, and I will live among them.
9 Make the tabernacle and all its furniture exactly as I show you on the pattern.
10 Have them build an ark of acacia wood. It must be two and a half cubits long, one and a half cubits wide, and one and a half cubits high.
11 Cover it with pure gold inside and out. Make a gold border around it.
12 Make four gold rings for it. Attach them to its four feet — two rings on each side.
13 Make poles of acacia wood and cover them with gold.
14 Put the poles through the rings on the sides of the ark. Use them to carry it.
15 The poles must stay in the rings of the ark. They must not be taken out.
16 Put inside the ark the Testimony that I will give you.
17 Make a mercy seat (atonement cover) of pure gold. It must be two and a half cubits long and one and a half cubits wide.
18 Make two cherubim of hammered gold at the two ends of the mercy seat.
19 Make one cherub at one end and one cherub at the other end. All of it must be made from one piece of gold.
20 The cherubim must have wings that spread upward. The wings must cover the mercy seat. The two cherubim must face each other, looking toward the mercy seat.
21 Set the mercy seat on top of the ark. Put the Testimony that I will give you inside the ark.
22 I will meet with you there, above the mercy seat. From between the two cherubim that are over the ark of the Testimony, I will speak to you. I will give you all my commands for the Israelites.
23 Also make a table of acacia wood. It must be two cubits long, one cubit wide, and one and a half cubits high.
24 Cover it with pure gold and make a gold border around it.
25 Make a rim around it, about a hand wide (3 inches). Put a gold border on the rim.
26 Make four gold rings for the table. Attach them to the four corners, at its four legs.
27 The rings must be close to the rim. They will hold the poles used to carry the table.
28 Make the poles of acacia wood and cover them with gold. The table will be carried with them.
29 Also make the plates and dishes for the table. Make the pitchers and bowls for pouring drink offerings. Make all of them from pure gold.
30 Place the Bread of the Presence on the table before me at all times.
31 Make a lampstand (menorah) of pure, hammered gold. It must be all one piece — its base, shaft, cups, buds, and petals.
32 Six branches must extend from the sides of the lampstand — three on one side and three on the other.
33 Three cups shaped like almond blossoms must be on the first branch. Each cup must have a bud and a petal. Do the same for all six branches that extend from the lampstand.
34 On the lampstand itself, there must be four cups shaped like almond blossoms, with buds and petals.
35 Place a bud under each pair of branches that extend from the lampstand. There must be three buds for the three pairs of branches.
36 The buds and branches must all be one piece with the lampstand. The whole thing must be hammered out of pure gold.
37 Make seven lamps and set them on the lampstand. They must give light to the area in front of it.
38 The wick trimmers and their trays must be of pure gold.
39 Use a talent of pure gold (about 75 pounds) for the lampstand and all these tools.
40 See to it that you make everything according to the pattern shown to you on the mountain.

title: Exodus 26
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 "Make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine linen. Use blue, purple, and scarlet yarn. Have cherubim woven into them by a skilled worker.
2 Each curtain must be twenty-eight cubits long and four cubits wide. All the curtains must be the same size.
3 Join five of the curtains together. Join the other five together as well.
4 Make loops of blue cloth on the edge of the last curtain in the first set. Do the same for the last curtain in the second set.
5 Make fifty loops on one curtain and fifty loops on the matching curtain. The loops must line up across from each other.
6 Then make fifty gold clasps. Use them to join the curtains together. So the tabernacle will be one piece.
7 Make curtains of goat hair for the tent over the tabernacle. There will be eleven curtains in all.
8 Each of the eleven curtains must be the same size — thirty cubits long and four cubits wide.
9 Join five curtains into one set and the other six into another set. Then fold the sixth curtain over double at the front of the tent.
10 Make fifty loops along the edge of the last curtain in the first set. Make fifty loops along the edge of the matching curtain in the second set.
11 Make fifty bronze clasps. Put them through the loops to join the tent together as one piece.
12 Some of the tent curtain will hang over. Let the extra half curtain hang down over the back of the tabernacle.
13 The tent curtains will be one cubit longer on each side. The extra cloth will hang over the sides of the tabernacle to cover it.
14 Also make a covering for the tent from ram skins dyed red. Over that, put a covering of fine leather.
15 Make upright frames of acacia wood for the tabernacle.
16 Each frame must be ten cubits long and one and a half cubits wide.
17 Each frame must have two pegs joined to each other. Make all the frames of the tabernacle this way.
18 Make twenty frames for the south side of the tabernacle.
19 Put forty silver bases under the twenty frames. There will be two bases for each frame, one under each peg.
20 For the second side, the north side of the tabernacle, make twenty frames
21 and forty silver bases — two bases under each frame.
22 Make six frames for the rear of the tabernacle, the west side.
23 Make two frames for the two back corners of the tabernacle.
24 Join them together from bottom to top, fitted into a single ring. These will be the two corners.
25 So there will be eight frames and sixteen silver bases — two under each frame.
26 Make five crossbars of acacia wood for the frames on one side of the tabernacle.
27 Make five for the other side, and five for the rear side of the tabernacle, on the west.
28 The middle crossbar must run through the center of the frames from end to end.
29 Cover the frames with gold. Make gold rings to hold the crossbars. Also cover the crossbars with gold.
30 Set up the tabernacle just like the pattern God showed you on the mountain.
31 Make a veil of blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and fine linen. Have cherubim woven into it by a skilled worker.
32 Hang it with gold hooks on four posts of acacia wood. Cover the posts with gold. They will stand on four silver bases.
33 Hang the veil from the clasps. Place the ark of the Testimony behind the veil. So the veil will separate the Holy Place from the Most Holy Place.
34 Put the mercy seat on the ark of the Testimony in the Most Holy Place.
35 Place the table outside the veil, on the north side of the tabernacle. Put the lampstand across from the table, on the south side.
36 For the entrance to the tent, make a curtain. Use blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and fine linen, with fine needlework.
37 Make five posts of acacia wood for the curtain. Cover them with gold and add gold hooks. Cast five bronze bases for them."

title: Exodus 27
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 "You must build an altar of acacia wood. The altar must be square. It must be five cubits long, five cubits wide, and three cubits high.
2 Make a horn at each of its four corners. The horns must be one piece with the altar. Then cover it with bronze.
3 Make all its tools of bronze. This includes pots to remove ashes, shovels, bowls for sprinkling, meat forks, and firepans.
4 Make a grating of bronze mesh for the altar. Put a bronze ring at each of the four corners of the mesh.
5 Set the grating under the edge of the altar. The mesh must reach halfway up the altar.
6 Also make poles of acacia wood for the altar. Cover them with bronze.
7 Put the poles through the rings. The poles will be on two sides of the altar when people carry it.
8 Build the altar with boards so that it is hollow inside. Make it just as I showed you on the mountain.
9 You must also make a courtyard for the tabernacle. On the south side, make curtains of fine linen, a hundred cubits long.
10 This side will have twenty posts and twenty bronze bases. The posts will have silver hooks and silver bands.
11 The north side must be the same. It will have curtains a hundred cubits long, twenty posts, and twenty bronze bases. The posts will have silver hooks and silver bands.
12 The west side of the courtyard will be fifty cubits wide. It will have ten posts and ten bases.
13 The east side of the courtyard, toward the sunrise, will also be fifty cubits wide.
14 Make the curtains on one side of the entrance fifteen cubits long. They will have three posts and three bases.
15 The curtains on the other side will also be fifteen cubits long, with three posts and three bases.
16 The entrance to the courtyard will be twenty cubits long. It will have a curtain decorated with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and fine linen. It will have four posts and four bases.
17 All the posts around the courtyard will have silver bands, silver hooks, and bronze bases.
18 The whole courtyard will be a hundred cubits long and fifty cubits wide. The curtains of fine linen will be five cubits high. The bases will be bronze.
19 All the tools of the tabernacle, for every use, must be made of bronze. This includes all its tent pegs and the tent pegs of the courtyard.
20 You must command the Israelites to bring you pure oil from pressed olives for the light. The oil will keep the lamps burning all the time.

21 In the Tent of Meeting, Aaron and his sons must take care of the lamps. They will do this outside the veil that stands in front of the Testimony. They will tend the lamps before the LORD from evening until morning. This is a lasting rule among the Israelites for all the generations to come.

title: Exodus 28
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 "Bring your brother Aaron to you from among the Israelites. Bring his sons too — Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar. They will serve me as priests.
2 Make sacred garments for your brother Aaron. These clothes will give him glory and beauty.
3 Speak to all the skilled workers. I have filled them with a spirit of wisdom. They will make garments to set Aaron apart. Then he can serve me as priest.

4 These are the garments they must make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, a woven tunic, a turban, and a sash. They will make these holy clothes for your brother Aaron and his sons. Then they may serve me as priests.
5 They will use gold, and blue, purple, and red yarn, and fine linen.
6 They must make the ephod of fine linen. They will weave it with gold and with blue, purple, and red yarn.
7 It must have two shoulder pieces. These are joined at the two corners so the ephod can be fastened.
8 The woven waistband of the ephod must be of one piece. It must be made the same way — with gold, with blue, purple, and red yarn, and with fine linen.
9 Take two onyx stones. Engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel.
10 Put six names on one stone and six names on the other. Use the order of their birth.
11 Engrave the names of the sons of Israel on the two stones. Do this the way a gem cutter cuts a seal. Then mount the stones in gold settings.
12 Fasten both stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod. They will be memorial stones for the sons of Israel. Aaron is to bear the names on his two shoulders as a memorial before the LORD.
13 Make gold settings,
14 and two chains of pure gold, made of braided cord. Attach these chains to the settings.
15 You must also make a breastpiece of judgment. Make it the same way as the ephod. Use gold, blue, purple, and red yarn, and fine linen.
16 It must be square when folded double. It must be a span long and a span wide.
17 Mount four rows of gemstones on it. In the first row, put a ruby, a topaz, and an emerald.
18 In the second row, put a turquoise, a sapphire, and a diamond.
19 In the third row, put a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst.
20 In the fourth row, put a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper. Mount these stones in gold settings.
21 There will be twelve stones for the twelve sons of Israel. Each stone is engraved like a seal with the name of one tribe.
22 For the breastpiece, make braided chains like cords of pure gold.
23 You must also make two gold rings. Fasten them to the two corners of the breastpiece.
24 Then fasten the two gold chains to the two gold rings at the corners of the breastpiece.
25 Fasten the other ends of the chains to the two gold settings. Attach them to the shoulder pieces of the ephod at the front.
26 Make two more gold rings. Attach them to the other two corners of the breastpiece, on the inside edge next to the ephod.
27 Make two more gold rings. Attach them to the bottom of the two shoulder pieces of the ephod. Put them in the front, near the seam just above the woven waistband.
28 Tie the rings of the breastpiece to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord. Then the breastpiece will stay above the waistband. It will not swing away from the ephod.
29 When Aaron enters the Holy Place, he must bear the names of the sons of Israel over his heart on the breastpiece of judgment. This is a steady reminder before the LORD.

30 Place the Urim and Thummim in the breastpiece of judgment. They will be over Aaron's heart when he comes before the LORD. Aaron will always carry the judgment of the sons of Israel over his heart before the LORD.
31 You must make the robe of the ephod from blue cloth only.
32 It must have an opening at the top in the center. A woven collar will go around the opening, like the opening of a garment. Then it will not tear.
33 Make pomegranates of blue, purple, and red yarn. Put them all around the lower hem. Place gold bells between them.
34 Place the gold bells and pomegranates one after the other around the lower hem of the robe.
35 Aaron must wear the robe when he serves. The sound will be heard when he goes in and out of the Holy Place before the LORD, so that he will not die.
36 You must make a plate of pure gold. Engrave on it as on a seal: HOLY TO THE LORD.
37 Fasten a blue cord to it to attach it to the turban. It must be on the front of the turban.

38 Aaron will wear it on his forehead. He will bear the guilt of the holy things that the sons of Israel give. These are all their holy gifts. The plate must always be on his forehead. Then the gifts will be accepted before the LORD.
39 You must weave the tunic with fine linen. Make the turban of fine linen. Make a sash with fine needlework.
40 Make tunics, sashes, and headbands for Aaron's sons. These will give them glory and beauty.
41 Put these garments on your brother Aaron and his sons. Then anoint them, ordain them, and set them apart. So they may serve me as priests.
42 Make linen undergarments to cover their bare skin. These must reach from the waist to the thigh.

43 Aaron and his sons must wear them when they enter the Tent of Meeting. They must also wear them when they come near the altar to serve in the Holy Place. Then they will not be guilty and die. This is a lasting rule for Aaron and his children after him."

title: Exodus 29
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 "This is what you must do to set apart Aaron and his sons as priests for me. Take a young bull and two rams without defect.
2 Also take bread without yeast, cakes without yeast mixed with oil, and wafers without yeast spread with oil. Make them all of fine wheat flour.
3 Put them in a basket. Bring them in the basket, along with the bull and the two rams.
4 Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Wash them with water.
5 Take the garments. Clothe Aaron with the tunic, the robe of the ephod, the ephod itself, and the breastplate. Tie the ephod on him with its woven waistband.
6 Put the turban on his head. Attach the holy crown to the turban.
7 Then take the anointing oil. Anoint him by pouring it on his head.
8 Bring his sons forward too. Clothe them with tunics.
9 Wrap sashes around Aaron and his sons. Tie headbands on them. The priesthood will be theirs by a law that lasts forever. This is how you must ordain Aaron and his sons.
10 Bring the bull to the front of the Tent of Meeting. Aaron and his sons must lay their hands on its head.
11 You must kill the bull before the LORD at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
12 Take some of the blood of the bull. Put it on the horns of the altar with your finger. Then pour out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.
13 Take all the fat that covers the inside parts. Take the lobe of the liver and both kidneys with the fat on them. Burn them on the altar.
14 But burn the meat of the bull, its hide, and its dung outside the camp. It is a sin offering.
15 Take one of the rams. Aaron and his sons must lay their hands on its head.
16 You must kill the ram. Take its blood and sprinkle it on all sides of the altar.
17 Cut the ram into pieces. Wash the inside parts and the legs. Place them with its head and the other pieces.
18 Then burn the entire ram on the altar. It is a burnt offering to the LORD. It is a pleasing aroma, a food offering to the LORD.
19 Take the second ram. Aaron and his sons must lay their hands on its head.

20 Kill the ram. Take some of its blood. Put it on the right earlobes of Aaron and his sons, on the thumbs of their right hands, and on the big toes of their right feet. Sprinkle the rest of the blood on all sides of the altar.

21 Take some of the blood on the altar and some of the anointing oil. Sprinkle it on Aaron and his garments, and also on his sons and their garments. Then he and his garments will be made holy, along with his sons and their garments.
22 Take the fat from the ram. Take the fat tail, the fat covering the inside parts, the lobe of the liver, both kidneys with the fat on them, and the right thigh. This is the ordination ram.
23 Also take one loaf of bread, one cake of bread made with oil, and one wafer. Take them from the basket of bread without yeast that is before the LORD.
24 Put all these in the hands of Aaron and his sons. Wave them before the LORD as a wave offering.
25 Then take them from their hands. Burn them on the altar on top of the burnt offering. It is a pleasing aroma before the LORD, a food offering to the LORD.
26 Take the breast of the ordination ram of Aaron. Wave it before the LORD as a wave offering. It will be your share.
27 Set apart for Aaron and his sons the breast of the wave offering that is waved. Also set apart the thigh of the heave offering that is lifted up from the ordination ram.
28 This will belong to Aaron and his sons as a regular share from the Israelites. It is the heave offering the Israelites must make to the LORD from their peace offerings.
29 The holy garments of Aaron will belong to his sons after him. They will be anointed and ordained in them.
30 The son who takes his place as priest must wear them for seven days. He will enter the Tent of Meeting to serve in the Holy Place.
31 Take the ordination ram and boil its meat in a holy place.
32 At the entrance to the Tent of Meeting, Aaron and his sons must eat the meat of the ram and the bread in the basket.
33 They must eat those things that made atonement for their ordination and for setting them apart. But no outsider may eat them, because these things are holy.
34 If any of the meat of the ordination or any of the bread is left until morning, burn the rest. It must not be eaten, because it is holy.
35 This is what you must do for Aaron and his sons, just as I have commanded you. Take seven days to ordain them.
36 Each day, offer a bull as a sin offering for atonement. Purify the altar by making atonement for it. Anoint it to set it apart.
37 For seven days you must make atonement for the altar and set it apart. Then the altar will be most holy. Whatever touches the altar will be holy.
38 This is what you must offer on the altar every day: two lambs that are a year old.
39 Offer one lamb in the morning and the other at twilight.
40 With the first lamb, offer a tenth of an ephah of fine flour. Mix it with a quarter hin of oil from pressed olives. Also offer a drink offering of a quarter hin of wine.
41 Offer the second lamb at twilight. Use the same grain offering and drink offering as in the morning. It is a pleasing aroma, a food offering to the LORD.
42 For the generations to come, this burnt offering must be made regularly. It is at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting before the LORD. There I will meet you and speak with you.
43 I will also meet with the Israelites there. That place will be made holy by my glory.
44 So I will set apart the Tent of Meeting and the altar. I will also set apart Aaron and his sons to serve me as priests.
45 Then I will dwell among the Israelites and be their God.
46 They will know that I am the LORD their God. I brought them out of the land of Egypt so that I might dwell among them. I am the LORD their God."

title: Exodus 30
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 "You must also make an altar of incense. Make it from acacia wood.
2 It must be square. Make it one cubit long, one cubit wide, and two cubits high. Its horns must be in one piece with it.
3 Cover the top, all the sides, and the horns with pure gold. Make a gold border around it.
4 Make two gold rings under the border, on opposite sides. They will hold the poles to carry the altar.
5 Make the poles from acacia wood and cover them with gold.
6 Place the altar in front of the veil. The veil is before the ark of the Testimony. The mercy seat is over the Testimony. There I will meet with you.
7 Aaron must burn sweet-smelling incense on this altar every morning. He will burn it when he takes care of the lamps.
8 Aaron must also burn the incense in the evening, when he sets up the lamps. He must burn it regularly before the LORD, for all your generations.
9 Do not offer on this altar any incense that I have not allowed. Do not offer a burnt offering or a grain offering on it. Do not pour a drink offering on it.

10 Once a year Aaron must make atonement on the horns of the altar. Through all your generations, he must do this every year. He must use the blood of the sin offering for atonement. The altar is most holy to the LORD."
11 Then the LORD said to Moses,
12 "When you count the Israelites, each man must pay the LORD a ransom for his life. He must pay it when he is counted. Then no plague will fall on them when you count them.
13 Everyone who is counted must pay a half shekel. Use the sanctuary shekel, which weighs twenty gerahs. This half shekel is an offering to the LORD.
14 Every man twenty years old or older must give this offering to the LORD.
15 When you give the LORD this offering to pay for your lives, the rich must not give more than a half shekel. The poor must not give less.
16 Take this atonement money from the Israelites. Use it for the service of the Tent of Meeting. It will be a reminder for the Israelites before the LORD. It will pay for your lives."
17 And the LORD said to Moses,
18 "Make a bronze basin with a bronze stand for washing. Place it between the Tent of Meeting and the altar. Put water in it.
19 Aaron and his sons must wash their hands and feet in this water.

20 When they enter the Tent of Meeting, they must wash with water. When they come near the altar to serve by giving an offering by fire to the LORD, they must wash. They must do this so that they will not die.
21 They must wash their hands and feet so that they will not die. This is a lasting rule for Aaron and his children, for all generations to come."
22 Then the LORD said to Moses,
23 "Take the best spices: 500 shekels of liquid myrrh, half as much (250 shekels) of sweet cinnamon, and 250 shekels of sweet cane.
24 Also take 500 shekels of cassia. Use the sanctuary shekel. And take a hin of olive oil.
25 From these, make a holy anointing oil. It must be a sweet-smelling blend, made by an oil-maker. It will be a holy anointing oil.
26 Use this oil to anoint the Tent of Meeting and the ark of the Testimony.
27 Anoint the table and all its tools. Anoint the lampstand and its tools. Anoint the altar of incense,
28 the altar of burnt offering and all its tools, and the basin with its stand.
29 Set them apart as holy. Then they will be most holy. Whatever touches them will be holy.
30 Anoint Aaron and his sons. Set them apart to serve me as priests.
31 Tell the Israelites, 'This will be my holy anointing oil for all your generations.
32 It must not be poured on a common person. You must not make anything like it with the same recipe. It is holy, and you must treat it as holy.
33 Anyone who mixes oil like it, or puts it on a stranger, must be cut off from his people.'"
34 The LORD also said to Moses, "Take sweet-smelling spices — gum resin, onycha, galbanum, and pure frankincense. Take equal amounts of each.
35 Make a sweet-smelling blend of incense, made by an oil-maker. Mix it with salt. It must be pure and holy.
36 Crush some of it into fine powder. Place it in front of the Testimony in the Tent of Meeting. There I will meet with you. It must be most holy to you.
37 You must never make incense for yourselves with this recipe. You must treat it as holy to the LORD.
38 Anyone who makes incense like it to enjoy its smell must be cut off from his people."

title: Exodus 31
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Look, I have chosen Bezalel by name. He is the son of Uri, son of Hur, from the tribe of Judah.
3 I have filled him with the Spirit of God. I have given him wisdom, understanding, knowledge, and all kinds of skills.
4 He can design beautiful works in gold, silver, and bronze.
5 He can cut gemstones to set in place. He can carve wood. He is skilled in every kind of work.
6 I have also chosen Oholiab son of Ahisamak, from the tribe of Dan, as his helper. I have given skills to all the skilled workers. They will make everything I have commanded you:
7 the Tent of Meeting, the ark of the Testimony with the mercy seat on it, and all the other tent furniture —
8 the table with its tools, the pure gold lampstand with all its tools, the altar of incense,
9 the altar of burnt offering with all its tools, and the basin with its stand —
10 also the woven clothes, both the holy clothes for Aaron the priest and the clothes for his sons to serve as priests,
11 and the anointing oil and sweet-smelling incense for the Holy Place. They must make everything just as I have commanded you."
12 And the LORD said to Moses,
13 "Tell the Israelites, 'You must keep my Sabbaths. This is a sign between me and you for the generations to come. So you may know that I am the LORD, who makes you holy.
14 Keep the Sabbath, for it is holy to you. Anyone who treats it as common must be put to death. Whoever does any work on that day must be cut off from his people.
15 For six days work may be done. But the seventh day is a Sabbath of complete rest, holy to the LORD. Whoever does any work on the Sabbath day is to be put to death.
16 The Israelites must keep the Sabbath. They must celebrate it as a lasting covenant for all generations.
17 It is a sign between me and the Israelites forever. For six days the LORD made the heavens and the earth. And on the seventh day he rested and was refreshed.'"
18 When the LORD finished speaking with Moses on Mount Sinai, he gave him the two tablets of the covenant law. They were tablets of stone, written by the finger of God.

title: Exodus 32
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 The people saw that Moses stayed long on the mountain. They gathered around Aaron and said, "Come, make us gods who will go before us. As for this fellow Moses who brought us up out of Egypt, we don't know what has happened to him."
2 Aaron said to them, "Take off the gold earrings from your wives, sons, and daughters. Bring them to me."
3 So all the people took off their gold earrings. They brought them to Aaron.
4 He took the gold from their hands. With a tool he shaped it into a calf of cast metal. And they said, "These are your gods, O Israel, who brought you up out of Egypt!"
5 When Aaron saw this, he built an altar in front of the calf. He announced, "Tomorrow there will be a festival to the LORD."
6 Early the next day, the people offered burnt offerings. They also brought fellowship offerings. Then they sat down to eat and drink. And they got up to party wildly.
7 Then the LORD said to Moses, "Go down at once. Your people, whom you brought up out of Egypt, have become corrupt.

8 They have turned away from my way so quickly. They have made a calf of cast metal for themselves. They have bowed down to it and offered sacrifices to it. They have said, 'These are your gods, O Israel, who brought you up out of Egypt.'"
9 The LORD also said to Moses, "I have seen this people. They are a stiff-necked people.
10 Now leave me alone, so that my anger may burn against them and destroy them. Then I will make you into a great nation."
11 But Moses sought the favor of the LORD his God. He said, "LORD, why should your anger burn against your people? You brought them out of Egypt with great power and a mighty hand.
12 Why should the Egyptians say, 'It was with evil intent that he brought them out, to kill them in the mountains'? Turn from your fierce anger. Relent and do not bring this harm on your people.

13 Remember your servants Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, to whom you swore by your own self. You said, 'I will make your descendants as many as the stars in the sky. I will give them all this land forever.'"
14 Then the LORD relented and did not bring on his people the disaster he had threatened.
15 Then Moses turned and went down the mountain. He carried the two tablets of the covenant law in his hands. They were written on both sides, front and back.
16 The tablets were the work of God. The writing was the writing of God, carved on the tablets.
17 Joshua heard the noise of the people shouting. He said to Moses, "There is the sound of war in the camp."
18 But Moses replied, "It is not the cry of victory. It is not the cry of defeat. I hear the sound of singing."
19 As Moses came near the camp, he saw the calf and the dancing. He burned with anger. He threw the tablets out of his hands and broke them at the foot of the mountain.
20 Then he took the calf they had made. He burned it in the fire and ground it to powder. He scattered the powder over the water. Then he made the Israelites drink it.
21 Moses asked Aaron, "What did this people do to you? Why did you lead them into such a great sin?"
22 "Do not be angry, my lord," Aaron replied. "You know that these people are set on evil.
23 They said to me, 'Make us gods who will go before us. As for this fellow Moses who brought us up out of Egypt, we don't know what has happened to him.'
24 So I said to them, 'Whoever has gold, take it off.' They gave it to me. I threw it into the fire, and out came this calf!"
25 Moses saw that the people were out of control. Aaron had let them run wild. They had become a joke to their enemies.
26 So Moses stood at the entrance to the camp. He said, "Whoever is for the LORD, come to me." And all the Levites gathered around him.

27 He told them, "This is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says: 'Each of you, put your sword at your side. Go through the camp from gate to gate. Kill your brother, your friend, and your neighbor.'"
28 The Levites did as Moses commanded. That day about three thousand people fell dead.
29 Then Moses said, "You have been set apart to the LORD today. Each man went against his son and his brother. So the LORD has given you a blessing this day."
30 The next day Moses said to the people, "You have committed a great sin. Now I will go up to the LORD; perhaps I can make atonement for your sin."
31 So Moses returned to the LORD. He said, "Oh, what a great sin these people have committed! They have made gods of gold for themselves.
32 But now, please forgive their sin. But if not, then blot me out of the book you have written."
33 The LORD replied to Moses, "Whoever has sinned against me I will blot out of my book.
34 Now go. Lead the people to the place I told you about. Look, my angel will go before you. But on the day I settle accounts, I will punish them for their sin."
35 And the LORD sent a plague on the people. This was because of what they had done with the calf that Aaron had made.

title: Exodus 33
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 The LORD said to Moses, "Leave this place. Take the people you brought out of Egypt. Go up to the land I promised. I promised it to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. I said, 'I will give it to your children.'
2 I will send an angel ahead of you. I will drive out the Canaanites, Amorites, Hittites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites.
3 Go up to a land full of milk and honey. But I will not go with you, because you are a stiff-necked people and I might destroy you on the way."
4 When the people heard these sad words, they cried with deep sadness. No one put on any jewelry.

5 For the LORD had said to Moses, "Tell the Israelites, 'You are a stiff-necked people. If I went with you for even one moment, I would destroy you. Now take off your jewelry. Then I will decide what to do with you.'"
6 So the Israelites took off their jewelry at Mount Horeb. They did not wear it again.
7 Moses used to take a tent and set it up outside the camp, far away. He called it the Tent of Meeting. Anyone asking the LORD a question would go to the Tent of Meeting outside the camp.
8 Whenever Moses went out to the tent, all the people stood up. They stood at the doors of their own tents. They watched Moses until he went inside.
9 As Moses entered the tent, a tall cloud came down. It stayed at the door. Then the LORD spoke with Moses.
10 When the people saw the tall cloud at the tent door, they stood up. Each one worshiped at the door of his own tent.
11 The LORD would speak to Moses face to face, as a friend speaks to a friend. Then Moses would return to the camp. But his young helper Joshua son of Nun stayed in the tent.
12 Then Moses said to the LORD, "Look, you have told me, 'Lead this people up.' But you have not told me whom you will send with me. Yet you have said, 'I know you by name, and you have pleased me.'
13 Now if I have truly pleased you, please show me your ways. Then I will know you and please you more. Remember that this nation is your people."
14 The LORD answered, "My Presence will go with you. I will give you rest."
15 Moses replied, "If your Presence does not go with us, do not send us up from here.
16 How will anyone know that you are pleased with me and with your people unless you go with us? What else will distinguish me and your people from all the other people on the face of the earth?"
17 The LORD said to Moses, "I will do the very thing you have asked, because I am pleased with you and I know you by name."
18 Then Moses said, "Now show me your glory."

19 The LORD replied, "I will cause all my goodness to pass in front of you, and I will proclaim my name, the LORD, in your presence. I will have mercy on whom I will have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I will have compassion."
20 But he added, "But you cannot see my face, for no one may see me and live."
21 The LORD continued, "There is a place near me. You will stand on a rock.
22 When my glory passes by, I will put you in a crack in the rock. I will cover you with my hand until I have passed by.
23 Then I will take my hand away. You will see my back, but you must not see my face."

title: Exodus 34
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses, "Cut two stone tablets like the first ones. I will write on them the words that were on the first tablets, which you broke.
2 Be ready in the morning. Come up Mount Sinai to meet me on the mountaintop.
3 No one may go up with you. No one may be seen anywhere on the mountain. Even the flocks and herds may not graze in front of it."
4 So Moses cut two stone tablets like the first ones. He got up early in the morning. He took the two stone tablets in his hands and climbed Mount Sinai, as the LORD had commanded.
5 The LORD came down in a cloud. He stood there with Moses and proclaimed his name, the LORD.
6 Then the LORD passed in front of Moses and called out: "The LORD, the LORD, the compassionate and gracious God, slow to anger, abounding in love and faithfulness,

7 maintaining love to thousands, and forgiving wickedness, rebellion and sin. Yet he does not leave the guilty unpunished. He punishes the children and their children for the sin of the parents to the third and fourth generation."
8 At once Moses bowed down to the ground and worshiped.
9 "O Lord," he said, "if I have found favor in your eyes, then let the Lord go with us. Although this is a stiff-necked people, forgive our wickedness and our sin, and take us as your inheritance."

10 And the LORD said, "Look, I am making a covenant with you. Before all your people I will do wonders never before done in any nation in all the world. All the people around you will see the work of the LORD. The thing I am doing with you is awesome.
11 Obey what I command you today. I will drive out before you the Amorites, Canaanites, Hittites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites.
12 Be careful. Do not make a covenant with the peoples of the land you are entering. They will become a trap among you.
13 Instead, break down their altars. Smash their sacred stones. Cut down their sacred poles.
14 Do not worship any other god, for the LORD, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God.
15 Do not make a covenant with the peoples of the land. They are not faithful and worship their gods. They sacrifice to them. They will invite you, and you will eat their sacrifices.
16 You will take some of their daughters as wives for your sons. Then their daughters will worship other gods. And they will lead your sons to do the same.
17 Do not make any cast idols for yourselves.
18 Keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days, at the set time in the month of Abib, eat bread without yeast, as I commanded you. For in the month of Abib you came out of Egypt.
19 The first child of every mother belongs to me. This includes every firstborn male among your animals — cattle or sheep.
20 Buy back the firstborn of a donkey with a lamb. If you do not buy it back, break its neck. Buy back all the firstborn of your sons. No one shall appear before me empty-handed.
21 Six days you shall work. But on the seventh day you shall rest. Even in the seasons of plowing and harvest, you must rest.
22 Celebrate the Feast of Weeks with the firstfruits of the wheat harvest. Celebrate the Feast of Ingathering at the turn of the year.
23 Three times a year all your men are to appear before the Lord GOD, the God of Israel.
24 I will drive out the nations before you. I will make your borders wider. No one will want your land when you go up three times a year to appear before the LORD your God.
25 Do not offer the blood of a sacrifice with anything that has yeast. Do not let any of the Passover Feast sacrifice remain until morning.
26 Bring the best of the firstfruits of your soil to the house of the LORD your God. Do not cook a young goat in its mother's milk."
27 The LORD also said to Moses, "Write down these words. For with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel."
28 So Moses was there with the LORD for forty days and forty nights. He did not eat bread or drink water. He wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant — the Ten Commandments.
29 Then Moses came down from Mount Sinai. He had the two tablets of the Testimony in his hands. He did not know that his face was shining from speaking with the LORD.
30 Aaron and all the Israelites looked at Moses. They saw that his face was shining. They were afraid to come near him.
31 But Moses called out to them. So Aaron and all the leaders came back to him, and Moses spoke to them.
32 After this, all the Israelites came near. Moses commanded them to do everything the LORD had told him on Mount Sinai.
33 When Moses had finished speaking with them, he put a veil over his face.
34 But whenever Moses went in before the LORD to speak with him, he removed the veil. When he came out, he told the Israelites what he had been commanded.
35 The Israelites saw that the face of Moses was shining. So Moses would put the veil back over his face until he went in to speak with the LORD.

title: Exodus 35
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 Then Moses called all the people of Israel together. He said, "These are the things the LORD has commanded you to do.
2 For six days, you may work. But the seventh day will be your holy day. It is a Sabbath — a full day of rest to the LORD. Whoever does any work on it is to be put to death.
3 Do not even light a fire in any of your dwellings on the Sabbath day."
4 Moses also said to all the people of Israel, "This is what the LORD has commanded:
5 Take an offering from among you for the LORD. Let everyone who is willing bring an offering to the LORD — gold, silver, and bronze;
6 blue, purple, and scarlet yarn; fine linen and goat hair;
7 ram skins dyed red and sea cow skins; acacia wood;
8 olive oil for the light; spices for the anointing oil and for the sweet incense;
9 and onyx stones and other gemstones to set on the ephod and breastpiece.
10 Let every skilled worker among you come and make everything the LORD has commanded:
11 the tabernacle with its tent and covering, its clasps and frames, its crossbars, posts, and bases;
12 the ark with its poles and mercy seat, and the veil to cover it;
13 the table with its poles and all its tools, and the Bread of the Presence;
14 the lampstand for light with its parts and lamps and oil for the light;
15 the altar of incense with its poles; the anointing oil and the sweet incense; the curtain for the doorway at the entrance to the tabernacle;
16 the altar of burnt offering with its bronze grate, its poles, and all its tools; the basin with its stand;
17 the curtains of the courtyard with its posts and bases, and the curtain for the gate of the courtyard;
18 the tent pegs for the tabernacle and for the courtyard, with their ropes;
19 and the woven clothes for serving in the holy place — both the holy clothes for Aaron the priest and the clothes for his sons to serve as priests."
20 Then all the people of Israel left the presence of Moses.

21 And everyone whose heart moved them and whose spirit was willing came. They brought an offering to the LORD for the work on the Tent of Meeting. They also brought it for all its services and for the holy clothes.

22 So all who were willing, both men and women, came. They brought brooches and earrings, rings and necklaces, and all kinds of gold jewelry. They all presented their gold as a wave offering to the LORD.
23 Everyone who had blue, purple, or scarlet yarn brought it. Others brought fine linen, goat hair, ram skins dyed red, or sea cow skins.
24 All who could give silver or bronze brought it as a gift to the LORD. Everyone who had acacia wood for any part of the service brought it too.
25 Every skilled woman spun yarn with her hands. She brought what she had spun: blue, purple, or scarlet yarn, or fine linen.
26 And all the skilled women whose hearts were moved spun the goat hair.
27 The leaders brought onyx stones and other gemstones. These were to set on the ephod and breastpiece.
28 They also brought spices and olive oil for the light, for the anointing oil, and for the sweet incense.
29 So all the men and women of Israel whose hearts were willing brought a freewill offering to the LORD. They gave it for all the work the LORD had commanded them through Moses.
30 Then Moses said to the Israelites, "See, the LORD has called by name Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur of Judah.
31 He has filled him with the Spirit of God. He gave him skill, ability, and knowledge in all kinds of craft work.
32 Bezalel can design artistic works in gold, silver, and bronze.
33 He can cut gemstones for settings and carve wood. He is a master of every kind of artistic craft.
34 The LORD has also given him and Oholiab son of Ahisamak of Dan the ability to teach others.

35 He has filled them with skill to do every kind of work — as stone-cutters, designers, and cloth-makers in blue, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine linen, and as weavers. They are artistic designers of every kind of craft."

title: Exodus 36
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 "So Bezalel, Oholiab, and every skilled worker must do the work. The LORD has given them skill to build the sanctuary. They will do everything the LORD has commanded."
2 Then Moses called Bezalel, Oholiab, and every skilled worker. The LORD had gifted them. Their hearts moved them to come and do the work.
3 They received from Moses all the gifts the Israelites had brought. These gifts were for building the sanctuary. The people kept bringing gifts from the heart morning after morning.
4 All the skilled workers were doing the work on the sanctuary. Then they left their work
5 and said to Moses, "The people are bringing more than enough. We have more than we need for the work the LORD commanded."
6 So Moses gave an order. They sent a message through the whole camp. "No man or woman should bring any more gifts for the sanctuary." So the people stopped bringing more.
7 What they already had was more than enough for all the work.
8 All the skilled workers made the ten curtains for the tabernacle. They were made of fine linen. They used blue, purple, and red yarn. They wove cherubim into them with great skill.
9 Each curtain was twenty-eight cubits long and four cubits wide. All the curtains were the same size.
10 He joined five of the curtains together. He joined the other five together too.
11 He made loops of blue cloth on the edge of the end curtain in the first set. He did the same on the end curtain in the second set.
12 He made fifty loops on one curtain. He made fifty loops on the end curtain of the second set. The loops lined up opposite each other.
13 He also made fifty gold clasps. They joined the curtains together. So the tabernacle became one whole unit.
14 Then he made curtains of goat hair for the tent over the tabernacle. There were eleven curtains in all.
15 Each of the eleven curtains was the same size. They were thirty cubits long and four cubits wide.
16 He joined five of the curtains into one set. He joined the other six into another set.
17 He made fifty loops along the edge of the end curtain in the first set. He made fifty loops on the matching curtain in the second set.
18 He also made fifty bronze clasps. They joined the tent together as one unit.
19 He also made a covering for the tent from ram skins dyed red. Over that he put a covering of sea cow skins.
20 Next, he made upright frames of acacia wood for the tabernacle.
21 Each frame was ten cubits long and one and a half cubits wide.
22 Each frame had two pegs joined to each other. He made all the frames of the tabernacle this way.
23 He made twenty frames for the south side of the tabernacle.
24 He made forty silver bases to go under the twenty frames. There were two bases under each frame, one under each peg.
25 For the north side of the tabernacle, he made twenty more frames
26 and forty silver bases. There were two bases under each frame.
27 He made six frames for the back of the tabernacle, the west side.
28 He also made two frames for the two back corners of the tabernacle.
29 They were joined together from bottom to top and fitted into a single ring. He made both corners the same way.
30 So there were eight frames and sixteen silver bases. There were two bases under each frame.
31 He also made five crossbars of acacia wood for the frames on one side of the tabernacle.
32 He made five crossbars for the other side. He made five crossbars for the back side, to the west.
33 He made the middle crossbar run through the center of the frames. It went from one end to the other.
34 He covered the frames with gold. He made gold rings to hold the crossbars. He also covered the crossbars with gold.
35 Next, he made the veil from blue, purple, and red yarn, and fine linen. He wove cherubim into it with great skill.
36 He made four poles of acacia wood for the veil. He covered them with gold and made gold hooks. He cast four silver bases for the poles.
37 For the entrance to the tent, he made a curtain. It was made with blue, purple, and red yarn, and fine linen.
38 He made five poles with their hooks. He covered the tops of the poles and their bands with gold. Their five bases were bronze.

title: Exodus 37
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood. It was two and a half cubits long. It was a cubit and a half wide. It was a cubit and a half high.
2 He covered it with pure gold, inside and out. He also made a gold molding around it.
3 He made four gold rings for its four feet. He put two rings on one side and two on the other.
4 Then he made poles of acacia wood. He covered them with gold.
5 He put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark. The poles were used to carry it.
6 He made a mercy seat, the atonement cover, of pure gold. It was two and a half cubits long and a cubit and a half wide.
7 He made two cherubim of beaten gold. He set them at the two ends of the mercy seat.
8 One cherub was on one end. The other cherub was on the other end. He made them from one piece of gold with the mercy seat.
9 The cherubim had wings that spread upward. Their wings covered the mercy seat. The cherubim faced each other and looked toward the mercy seat.
10 He also made the table for the bread of the Presence. It was acacia wood, two cubits long, a cubit wide, and a cubit and a half high.
11 He covered it with pure gold. He made a gold molding around it.
12 He made a rim around it, a handbreadth wide. He put a gold molding on the rim.
13 He made four gold rings for the table. He fastened them to the four corners at the four legs.
14 The rings were placed close to the rim. They held the poles used to carry the table.
15 He made the poles of acacia wood. He covered them with gold. They were used to carry the table.
16 He also made the things used at the table, all from pure gold. He made its plates and dishes. He made its bowls and pitchers for pouring drink offerings.
17 Then he made the lampstand of pure beaten gold. He made all of it from one piece — its base and central shaft, its cups, its buds, and its blossoms.
18 Six branches came out from the sides. Three were on one side, and three on the other.
19 Three cups shaped like almond blossoms were on the first branch, each with buds and blossoms. Three were on the next branch. It was the same for all six branches.
20 On the central shaft of the lampstand were four cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with buds and blossoms.
21 A bud was under the first pair of branches. A bud was under the second pair. A bud was under the third pair.
22 The buds and the branches were all one piece with the lampstand. The whole thing was beaten from pure gold.
23 He also made its seven lamps, its wick trimmers, and its trays. All of these were pure gold.
24 He made the lampstand and all its tools from one talent of pure gold.
25 He made the altar of incense from acacia wood. It was square — a cubit long, a cubit wide, and two cubits high. Its horns were one piece with it.
26 He covered the top, all the sides, and the horns with pure gold. Then he made a gold molding around it.
27 He made two gold rings below the molding, on opposite sides. They held the poles used to carry it.
28 He made the poles of acacia wood. He covered them with gold.
29 He also made the holy anointing oil. He made the pure, sweet-smelling incense — the work of a perfumer.

title: Exodus 38
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 Bezalel made the altar of burnt offering from acacia wood. It was square — five cubits long, five cubits wide, and three cubits high.
2 He made a horn at each of its four corners. The horns and the altar were one piece. He covered the altar with bronze.
3 He made all the altar's tools of bronze — its pots, shovels, sprinkling bowls, meat forks, and firepans.
4 He made a bronze grating for the altar. It went under the ledge, halfway up from the bottom.
5 He cast four rings at the four corners of the bronze grating. The rings held the poles.
6 He made the poles of acacia wood and covered them with bronze.
7 Then he put the poles into the rings on the sides of the altar. They were used to carry it. He made the altar with boards so that it was hollow.
8 Next he made the bronze basin and its stand. He made them from the bronze mirrors of the women who served at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
9 Then he made the courtyard. The south side was a hundred cubits long. It had curtains of fine linen,
10 with twenty posts and twenty bronze bases. The hooks and bands on the posts were silver.
11 The north side was also a hundred cubits long. It had twenty posts and twenty bronze bases. The hooks and bands of the posts were silver.
12 The west side was fifty cubits long. It had curtains, with ten posts and ten bases. The hooks and bands of the posts were silver.
13 The east side, toward the sunrise, was also fifty cubits long.
14 The curtains on one side of the entrance were fifteen cubits long. They had three posts and three bases.
15 The curtains on the other side were also fifteen cubits long. They had three posts and three bases as well.
16 All the curtains around the courtyard were made of fine linen.
17 The bases for the posts were bronze. The hooks and bands were silver. The plating for the tops of the posts was silver. So all the posts of the courtyard were banded with silver.
18 The curtain for the entrance to the courtyard was embroidered with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and fine linen. It was twenty cubits long. Like the courtyard curtains, it was five cubits high.
19 It had four posts and four bronze bases. Their hooks were silver. The bands and the plating of their tops were also silver.
20 All the tent pegs for the tabernacle and the courtyard around it were bronze.
21 This is the inventory for the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the Testimony. Moses gave the command. The Levites recorded it under the direction of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
22 Bezalel son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made everything the LORD had commanded Moses.
23 With him was Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan. He was an engraver, designer, and embroiderer in blue, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine linen.
24 All the gold from the wave offering, used for the work on the sanctuary, was 29 talents and 730 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel.
25 The silver from those counted in the people totaled 100 talents and 1,775 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel —
26 a beka per person, that is, half a shekel, by the sanctuary shekel. It came from every man twenty years old or older who crossed over to be counted — a total of 603,550 men.
27 The hundred talents of silver were used to cast the bases of the sanctuary and the bases of the veil — 100 bases from the 100 talents, one talent per base.
28 With the 1,775 shekels of silver, he made the hooks for the posts. He covered their tops and made bands for them.
29 The bronze from the wave offering was 70 talents and 2,400 shekels.
30 He used it to make the bases for the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. He also made the bronze altar, its bronze grating, and all the altar tools.
31 He made the bases for the courtyard around it and for its gate. He also made all the tent pegs for the tabernacle and the courtyard around it.

title: Exodus 39
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 From the blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, they made special woven clothes. These were for service in the sanctuary. They also made the holy clothes for Aaron, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
2 Bezalel made the ephod. He used gold, blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and fine linen.
3 They hammered the gold into thin sheets. Then they cut it into threads. They wove these threads with the blue, purple, and scarlet yarn and the fine linen. It was the work of a skilled worker.
4 They made shoulder pieces for the ephod. These were joined at its two corners, so it could be fastened.
5 The waistband of the ephod was made in one piece with it. It used the same skilled work — gold, blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and fine linen — just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
6 They set the onyx stones in gold settings. They cut into them like a seal with the names of the sons of Israel.
7 Then they fastened them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod. These were stones to remember the sons of Israel, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
8 He made the breastpiece with the same skilled work as the ephod. He used gold, blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and fine linen.
9 It was square when folded double. It was one span long and one span wide.
10 They set four rows of gemstones on it. The first row had a ruby, a topaz, and an emerald.
11 The second row had a turquoise, a sapphire, and a diamond.
12 The third row had a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst.
13 The fourth row had a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper. All these stones were set in gold settings.
14 The twelve stones matched the names of the sons of Israel. Each stone was cut like a seal with the name of one of the twelve tribes.
15 For the breastpiece they made twisted chains of pure gold, like cords.
16 They also made two gold settings and two gold rings. They fastened the two rings to the two top corners of the breastpiece.
17 Then they fastened the two gold chains to the two gold rings at the corners of the breastpiece.
18 They fastened the other ends of the two chains to the two gold settings. They attached these to the shoulder pieces of the ephod at the front.
19 They made two more gold rings. They put them on the other two corners of the breastpiece, on the inside edge next to the ephod.
20 They made two more gold rings. They attached them to the bottom of the two shoulder pieces of the ephod, in front, near the seam above the waistband.

21 Then they tied the rings of the breastpiece to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord. So the breastpiece sat above the waistband. It would not swing out from the ephod, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
22 They made the robe of the ephod all of blue cloth. It was the work of a weaver.
23 It had an opening in the center, like the neck of a coat. A collar went around the opening so it would not tear.
24 They made pomegranates on the lower hem of the robe. They used blue, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine linen.
25 They also made bells of pure gold. They put them around the hem between the pomegranates.
26 The bells and pomegranates went one after the other around the lower hem. The robe was for service, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
27 For Aaron and his sons they made tunics of fine linen. It was the work of a weaver.
28 They also made the turban of fine linen, the decorated headbands, and the underclothes of fine linen.
29 They made the sash of fine linen, with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn sewn in. They did this just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
30 They also made the gold plate of the holy crown out of pure gold. They cut writing on it like a seal: HOLY TO THE LORD.
31 Then they fastened a blue cord to it. This held it on the turban, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
32 So all the work for the tabernacle, the Tent of Meeting, was finished. The Israelites did everything just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
33 Then they brought the tabernacle to Moses. They brought the tent with all its things — its clasps, frames, crossbars, posts, and bases.
34 They brought the covering of ram skins dyed red, the covering of fine leather, and the veil.
35 They brought the ark of the Testimony with its poles, and the mercy seat.
36 They brought the table with all its tools and the Bread of the Presence.
37 They brought the pure gold lampstand with its lamps and tools, and the oil for the light.
38 They brought the gold altar, the anointing oil, the sweet incense, and the curtain for the entrance to the tent.
39 They brought the bronze altar with its bronze grating, poles, and all its tools. They brought the basin with its stand.

40 They brought the curtains of the courtyard with its posts and bases. They brought the curtain for the gate of the courtyard, its ropes and tent pegs, and all the things for the service of the tabernacle, the Tent of Meeting.
41 They also brought the woven clothes for service in the sanctuary — the holy clothes for Aaron the priest, and the clothes for his sons to serve as priests.
42 The Israelites had done all the work just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
43 Moses checked all the work. He saw that they had done it just as the LORD had commanded. So Moses blessed them.

title: Exodus 40
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Exodus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "On the first day of the first month set up the tabernacle, the tent of meeting.
3 Put the ark of the Testimony in it. Hide the ark behind the curtain.
4 Then bring in the table and set out its things. Also bring in the lampstand and set up its lamps.
5 Place the gold altar of incense in front of the ark of the Testimony. Hang the curtain at the entrance to the tabernacle.
6 Place the altar for burnt offerings in front of the entrance to the tabernacle, the tent of meeting.
7 Place the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar. Put water in it.
8 Set up the courtyard around it. Hang the curtain for the entrance to the courtyard.
9 Take the anointing oil. Anoint the tabernacle and everything in it. Set it apart as holy, with all its furnishings. It will be holy.
10 Anoint the altar for burnt offerings and all its tools. Set the altar apart as holy. It will be most holy.
11 Anoint the basin and its stand. Set them apart as holy.
12 Then bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting. Wash them with water.
13 Clothe Aaron with the holy clothes. Anoint him and set him apart as holy. Then he may serve me as a priest.
14 Bring his sons forward. Clothe them with tunics.
15 Anoint them just as you anointed their father. Then they too may serve me as priests. Their anointing will give them a priesthood that will never end, through all their family lines."
16 Moses did everything just as the LORD had commanded him.
17 So the tabernacle was set up on the first day of the first month, in the second year.
18 Moses set up the tabernacle. He laid its bases. He put up its frames. He pushed in its crossbars. He set up its posts.
19 Then he spread the tent over the tabernacle. He put the covering over the tent, just as the LORD had commanded him.
20 Moses took the Testimony and placed it in the ark. He attached the poles to the ark. He set the mercy seat on top of the ark.
21 Then he brought the ark into the tabernacle. He put up the curtain to hide the ark of the Testimony, just as the LORD had commanded him.
22 Moses placed the table in the tent of meeting, on the north side of the tabernacle, outside the curtain.
23 He arranged the bread on it before the LORD, just as the LORD had commanded him.
24 He placed the lampstand in the tent of meeting, opposite the table, on the south side of the tabernacle.
25 He set up the lamps before the LORD, just as the LORD had commanded him.
26 Moses placed the gold altar in the tent of meeting, in front of the curtain.
27 He burned sweet-smelling incense on it, just as the LORD had commanded him.
28 Then he put up the curtain at the entrance to the tabernacle.
29 He placed the altar for burnt offerings near the entrance to the tabernacle, the tent of meeting. He offered on it the burnt offering and the grain offering, just as the LORD had commanded him.
30 He placed the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar. He put water in it for washing.
31 Moses, Aaron, and his sons washed their hands and feet from it.
32 They washed whenever they entered the tent of meeting or came near the altar, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
33 Moses set up the courtyard around the tabernacle and the altar. He hung the curtain for the entrance to the courtyard. So Moses finished the work.
34 Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
35 Moses could not enter the tent of meeting because the cloud had settled on it, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
36 In all the travels of the Israelites, whenever the cloud lifted from above the tabernacle, they would set out.
37 But if the cloud did not lift, they did not set out — until the day it lifted.
38 So the cloud of the LORD was over the tabernacle by day, and fire was in the cloud by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel during all their travels.
